# Permissions and Safety

The agent has a deep permission model because it can read files, write files, run shell commands, and spawn worker agents.

## Permission Context

`Tool.ts` defines the permission context passed into tool execution. That context carries the current mode, allow/deny rules, additional working directories, and flags that control whether the agent may prompt the user.

## Permission Setup

`utils/permissions/permissionSetup.ts` computes the active permission rules from settings, feature gates, and the current operating mode. It also applies restrictions for dangerous commands and auto mode.

## Filesystem Safety

`utils/permissions/filesystem.ts` contains the path-level safety logic. It recognizes dangerous config files and directories, special `.claude/` paths, plan files, and other sensitive locations.

This is where the runtime prevents self-modifying or privilege-escalation-style behavior from being granted too casually.

## Why It Matters

Because the agent can execute shell and file operations, permissions are part of the architecture, not a peripheral concern. The system relies on explicit path validation, rule matching, and gating to keep automation bounded.
